name: Naval Station Great Lakes
tagline: The Navy's only boot camp and largest training installation, on the shore of Lake Michigan north of Chicago.
_note: Navy's only Recruit Training Command (boot camp) + many 'A' schools (Lake County IL, Lake Michigan, ~35 mi N of Chicago). CONUS full template (BAH MHA IL092 Great Lakes NAVTRACEN; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). Population = recruits + students + staff/instructors. Medical: Captain James A. Lovell Federal Health Care Center (joint VA-DoD). Facts verified from official sources (cnic.navy.mil NS Great Lakes, Lovell FHCC, DeCA/NEX, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Illinois moved to TRICARE West in 2024. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 60085 (Waukegan).

data.Location: North Chicago / Lake County, IL (Lake Michigan, ~35 mi N of Chicago)
data.Role: The Navy's only boot camp (Recruit Training Command) and largest training installation

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Naval Station Great Lakes is the home of Recruit Training Command, the Navy's only boot camp, where every new Sailor begins. It's also the Navy's largest training installation, hosting many 'A' schools for initial technical training.

On the shore of Lake Michigan about 35 miles north of Chicago, the population is a constant flow of recruits and students plus the staff and instructors who train them, set among Lake County's suburbs.

sections.title: Lake County Living, With Chicago Nearby

data.subtitle: For staff and instructors, the choice is which Lake County suburb fits your commute and schools, with Chicago's metro amenities within reach and four real seasons to plan for.
intro: Most family housing decisions here are for staff and instructors (recruits and many students live in barracks). Lake County offers a range of suburbs and prices, and on-base housing is available.
Where you live mostly comes down to commute and schools across the northern suburbs, balancing lakefront towns against more affordable inland areas, with Chicago a train ride away.

recommendations.advice: North Chicago, Waukegan, and Gurnee sit near the base for a short commute.
Libertyville, Gurnee, and the surrounding Lake County towns are popular with families for their schools.
On-base housing is available and convenient; weigh it against the local suburbs.
Expect real Midwest winters and lake-effect weather; budget for heating and winter driving.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID from the Visitor Control Center. Access can be busier around recruit graduations.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Naval Station Great Lakes site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Naval Station Great Lakes has privatized on-base family housing across several neighborhoods for staff and instructors (recruits and many students are berthed in barracks).
Eligibility and availability vary by rank and family size, so start with the housing office for the current manager, options, and waitlists.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA IL092 (Great Lakes NAVTRACEN)

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. North Chicago, Waukegan, Gurnee, and other Lake County districts serve military families depending on where you live.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the district and campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Hospital: Captain James A. Lovell Federal Health Care Center
data.Care: A unique joint VA-DoD facility providing primary, specialty, inpatient, and emergency care for service members, recruits, families, and veterans

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and at Great Lakes the call depends on your tour length and how settled you want to be in the suburbs.
rent: Your tour is short (about two years or less) or your orders feel uncertain.
You want time to learn Lake County before committing.
You'd rather not gamble on resale timing when you PCS out.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect to be here three or more years.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The Lake County market fits your budget for a home you like.
You've found a suburb and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer. Weigh your tour length and Illinois property taxes (which run high) against the benefits of owning. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book Navy Gateway Inns & Suites or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your command and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: Check on-base availability and get the housing office's read on Lake County before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and Illinois vehicle registration, and learn the visitor-pass process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the gaining district (North Chicago, Waukegan, Gurnee, or another Lake County district) early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment so you can be seen at the Lovell Federal Health Care Center.

data.q: What is Naval Station Great Lakes?
data.a: It's home to Recruit Training Command, the Navy's only boot camp, plus many 'A' schools. It's the Navy's largest training installation.
Staff and instructors live in on-base housing or across Lake County (North Chicago, Waukegan, Gurnee, Libertyville). Recruits and many students are berthed on base.

data.q: Is the medical facility different here?
data.a: Yes. The Captain James A. Lovell Federal Health Care Center is a unique joint VA-DoD facility serving both military and veteran populations.
Great Lakes is MHA IL092. Use the calculator above for your grade.
